steps.title: Know what infinitely many solutions means
steps.content: A system has infinitely many solutions only when both equations are the same line. The second equation must produce exactly the same $y$-values as $y=6x+3$ for every $x$.

steps.title: Look for an equivalent equation
steps.content:
Equivalent equations are made by doing the same operation to both sides. Starting with
$$y=6x+3$$
multiply both sides by $2$:
$$2y=2(6x+3).$$

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Choice D (‘$2(y)=2(6x+3)$’) is $2(y)=2(6x+3)$, which is exactly the original equation multiplied by $2$ on both sides.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D, $2y=2(6x+3)$, is just the original equation multiplied by $2$, so it describes the same line. The system therefore has infinitely many solutions. D is correct.
